title: waltheria incana
intro: Waltheria incana is a plant species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the family Malvaceae, commonly known as the hibiscus or mallow family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which have been attributed to its high content of flavonoids and saponins. The health benefits of Waltheria incana include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Waltheria incana is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or topical ointment, which can be obtained through the leaves, bark, and roots of the plant.
